Submit proof of settlement of the disallowances to the Audit Team for the issuance of NSSDC.

observations.recommendation: 1.5	We recommended that the Accountant drop from the books those items which were already disposed off through the destruction by Management. Accountable persons who were issued municipal properties which were lost or destroyed, against their will, shall seek relief from accountability from COA to free them from liability. Also, the Accountant shall furnish the Treasurer with the schedule of PPE, derived from ledgers, on which reconciliation with property cards of the latter shall be based upon. Adjust the books in accordance with the valuation of properties existing within the premises of the Municipality after establishing their rightful ownership thereto.

2.6	We recommended that Management should review the validity, propriety and legality of claims of prior years  as evidenced by stale checks in the reconciling items in the Cash-in-Bank account and if found valid and legal, these should be reversed to Accounts Payable and the payees be reissued check payment  to extinguish the obligation of the Municipality with these third parties. Moreover, the reconciling items on the checks paid to the former Municipal Treasurer and other parties which were already encashed at the bank but remained unrecorded in the books should be booked as Receivables on the payees and other parties who authorized the payment or are parties to the transactions. However, if these are found valid, vouchers and supporting documents should be reconstructed and recorded as items of prior years.

3.4	We recommended that Management reconcile the RPT and SET Receivables and Deferred RPT and SET Taxes by tracing the application of payments of previous periods from the Monthly Report of Collections of the Municipal Treasurer to the JEV and the General Journal. Determine the discrepancies and adjust the accounts so that these should reflect the correct amount of RPT and SET Receivables due from the taxpayers as of the end of FY 2016.

4.5	We recommended that Management review the existing policies, strategies and remedies of the Municipality in the collection of delinquent real property taxes; make massive campaign in the collection of real property taxes such as giving advance notices to the tax payers, discounts on surcharges and penalties, recognition or award for all taxpayers who are paying on time; exert more effort to reconcile with the records of the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to determine the correct balances of the delinquent real property taxpayers; make civil remedies as stipulated in this Code, such as: by administrative action through distraint of goods, chattels, or effects, and other personal property of whatever character, including stocks and other securities, debt, credits, bank accounts, and interest in and rights to personal property, and by levy upon real property and interest in or rights to real property; and by judicial action.

5.4	We recommended that Management (1)trace back previous entries and determine the cause of the discrepancies in recording and in failure to record transactions that affected the accounts; (2) adjust the affected accounts, and (3) submit the JEV to the Auditor to prevent the bloating of the Receivables in the consolidated financial statements.

6.5	We recommended that Management comply strictly with the above-cited COA  rules and regulations and prepare a semi-monthly schedule of cash advance payments to be withheld per accountable officer which shall be strictly implemented until full payment of accountabilities at the end of 2017.

7.4	We recommended that Management (1)analyze the composition of dormant receivables and prepare the individual or detailed schedule of the accounts; (2) determine the collectibility of the accounts and send collection letters to concerned individuals or their heirs; (3) exhaust all applicable legal remedies to collect; (4) request for write off from COA if remedies fail; and (5) effect adjustments in the books.

8.5	We recommended that Management prepare projected computations for the term of full settlement of disallowances per liable person, increase the amount of partial settlement thereof and strictly enforce the COA Order of Execution (COE) as provided in Section 7.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

9.5	We recommended that the Accountant prepare a schedule of officers and employees who were remitted salary loan repayments per lending institution. Inform these persons in writing that municipal funds were used to pay their loans and remind them that a Receivable shall be recorded in their account which shall be collected from them through payroll deduction. Afterwhich, deduct from the payrolls of concerned persons the amount due to be returned to the Municipality in the immediately next pay period.

10.4	We recommended that Management should account, return or liquidate the savings from national projects to the concerned agencies in order that the funds shall be used to further national development. Otherwise, request the concerned agencies for reprogramming of the funds into another project that would promote economic development in the area.

11.10	We recommended that Management refund the payments for rice subsidy, Christmas gift and medical assistance to prevent the loss of money from the municipal coffers. The officials of the employees’ association should register for  accreditation with the Civil Service Commission. They should execute a Collective Negotiation Agreement and state therein the stipulation to grant CNA Incentive that covers the medical and rice benefits of officials and employees for them to be legally entitled to payment thereof.

12.5	We recommended that Management should base their PS cap according to Section 325(a) of the Local Government Code in the preparation of the annual budget from which items of PS shall be planned. Stop the practice of appropriating more than what the law for PS limitation requires. Excess expenditures over the PS cap shall be subject for disallowance in conformity with the provisions of RA No. 7160.

13.5	We recommended that Management withholds 10% retention fee on all progress billings, partial and full payment on municipal projects to ensure that the contractor shall be held liable for all defects arising therefrom, or submit surety bond as an alternative control measure to support the final payment before the issuance of final acceptance of the project is made one year therefrom.

14.5	We recommended that Management refrain from disbursing out of the 20% Development Fund those expenditures which do not belong to its prescribed purposes. Management return to the Fund those paid expenditures which are contrary to what is prescribed thereunto in compliance with DBM-DILG JMC No. 2011-1. In this manner, the Municipality implements the 20% Development Fund to finance the socio-economic needs of its constituents.

15.4	We recommended that Management indicate the mode of procurement of items to be procured during the year in the Annual Procurement Plan of the Municipality at the beginning of the year as the basis for periodic procurements. Management should opt to conduct public bidding on all qualified procurements to save on appropriations to attain efficiency in the delivery of basic services or resort to alternative procurement as the case may be.

16.6	We recommended that the Disbursing Officer controls the release of RATA and shall only pay the municipal officials after the 17th working day of the month to ensure that the officials receiving the allowance have attended to work performance for a minimum of  17 working days and that whatever absences  they incur as of the end of the month shall be deducted from their respective RATA claims in accordance with the RATA schedule prescribed in LBC 103 and  to prevent violation of Sections 58 and  88 of PD No. 1445.

17.3	We recommended that Management through the HRMO submits the leave cards of the retiring employee to the Auditor for verification to ensure that the claimed number of days to be commuted for terminal leave benefits are proper, accurate and fair.

18.4	We recommended that the Human Resource Management Officer produce computer printed Daily Time Records of officials and employees every month based on Biometrics data to prove  the accuracy and reliability of attendance for the month and to encourage truthful reporting of the number of days worked as well as number of days of absences to be properly accounted for in the computation of salaries and wages. We recommend that Management comply with the various documentary requirements to transactions to prove the accuracy, validity and legality of money claims against municipal funds.

19.3	We recommended that drivers of municipal vehicles as well as construction and heavy equipment prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Report of Official Travels per vehicle to support the claims for fuel and lubricants pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 76-61.

20.4	We recommended that Management stop the granting of cash advances to municipal officials and employees who are not duly bonded to ensure the truthful disbursement of funds for their intended purposes and the proper safekeeping thereof.

21.6	We recommended that Management must provide sufficient manpower complement and equipment to attend to the implementation and supervision of municipal projects in order that it shall succeed in its mandate to contribute to poverty reduction and to the upliftment of the  general welfare of its constituents. Moreover, the presence of abled manpower shall capacitate the mobilization and supervision of projects  and prove the municipality’s track record on its efficient and effective utilization of scarce government resources.
